frameworks.name: UK Data (Use & Access) Act 2025 s.80 (formerly UK AI Bill Art 22c)

controls.title: Driver identity verification + tachograph card issuance
controls.description: Verify driver identity before card issuance; maintain tamper-evident audit trail of every issue + revocation event.
controls.uk_regulation: EC 165/2014 (Smart Tachograph 2) Art 26-28 + DVSA Operator Licence repute (Goods Vehicles Licensing of Operators Act 1995 s.13A)

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Card issuance is a high-trust identity-binding action; ISO 42001 requires verification + audit.
If AI assists card-issuance decisions, training data must be governed + records kept.
Identity-bound issuance is a governance + context-mapping concern.
Card = credential; CC6.1 logical-access controls apply.

controls.evidence_pattern: Signed attestation log of every card issue + revocation event with driver-id, card-id, issuer, timestamp, HMAC-SHA256 chain.

controls.title: Drivers' Hours monitoring + EU 561/2006 + AETR compliance
controls.description: Continuously monitor drivers' hours against EU 561/2006 + AETR limits; surface infringements before they become DVSA-reportable.
controls.uk_regulation: EU 561/2006 + Transport Act 1968 (Working Time) + AETR Agreement

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Hours-of-service monitoring IS the operational AI risk control.
Automated hours-monitoring requires human-in-the-loop for infringement decisions affecting drivers.
Ongoing infringement-detection = ongoing AI-risk management.
Continuous monitoring of a regulated process.

controls.evidence_pattern: Signed weekly hours-summary attestation per driver, with infringement flags + remediation actions logged.

controls.title: Daily walkaround vehicle defect reporting (driver pre-use check)
controls.description: Driver records daily walkaround check before first use; defects flagged + routed to fitter; signed nil-defect attestations build the audit trail.
controls.uk_regulation: DVSA Guide to Maintaining Roadworthiness s.3 + Operator Licence undertakings

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Pre-use check is a documented operational procedure.
Defect-discovery → maintenance-change workflow is a change-management control.

controls.evidence_pattern: Signed daily walkaround check per vehicle, with defect-id (if any), driver-id, location, photographs hashed into the attestation.

controls.title: DVSA Earned Recognition KPI feed integrity
controls.description: Continuous feed of KPIs to DVSA (drivers' hours, tachograph compliance, vehicle defects, etc.) with signed attestations proving feed integrity.
controls.uk_regulation: DVSA Earned Recognition Standards (Vehicle Maintenance + Drivers' Hours)

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: ER is by definition continuous monitoring + measurement.
ER feed is a commitment to availability of compliance data.

controls.evidence_pattern: HMAC-chained feed receipts with KPI snapshot, period, signing key id; auditor verifies chain integrity via public verifier.

controls.title: FORS Bronze → Silver → Gold accreditation dossier
controls.description: Auto-generate the audit-grade dossier for each FORS level from tacho/walkaround/training records; refresh on schedule for re-accreditation.
controls.uk_regulation: FORS Standard v6 (Management, Vehicles, Drivers, Operations)

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Bronze→Gold progression = staged operational maturity model.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-period signed dossier covering each FORS-Standard requirement, with cross-references to underlying signed evidence.

controls.title: CLOCS Standard 6 lift/site evidence
controls.description: Evidence-pack generation for CLOCS Standard 6 (vehicles + drivers + journeys + community-safety measures).
controls.uk_regulation: CLOCS Standard 6 (Construction Logistics + Community Safety)

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Standardised site-visit evidence collection.

controls.evidence_pattern: Signed per-site visit attestation: driver, vehicle, time-on-site, community-safety briefings completed.

controls.title: Direct Vision Standard (DVS) HGV certification
controls.description: Track DVS star-rating per HGV (≥12t) in London; ensure Progressive Safe System fitted where star rating < threshold.
controls.uk_regulation: TfL Direct Vision Standard + HGV Safety Permit Scheme

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: DVS rating is a documented risk-treatment for vulnerable-road-user incidents.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-HGV signed DVS-rating + Safe-System-fitment attestation, refreshed on TfL re-rating cycle.

controls.title: BS 7121 lift plan + appointed-person sign-off
controls.description: For every crane/hiab lift, generate a BS 7121 lift plan signed by an Appointed Person before the lift commences.
controls.uk_regulation: BS 7121-1:2016 + LOLER 1998 Reg 8 (Organisation of lifting operations)

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Lift planning is risk assessment + operational planning.
Each lift is a planned operational change.

controls.evidence_pattern: Signed lift plan: site, load, crane, AP name + competence cert, risk assessment, method statement, exclusion zone.

controls.title: LOLER 6/12-monthly thorough examination
controls.description: Schedule + record LOLER thorough examinations (6-monthly for accessory + passenger lifts; 12-monthly otherwise).
controls.uk_regulation: LOLER 1998 Reg 9 + Schedule 1

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Scheduled inspection cycle is monitoring + measurement of equipment integrity.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-equipment signed thorough-examination report including examiner-id, defects, next-examination date.

controls.title: ALLMI hiab operator certification
controls.description: Track ALLMI Operator Certification + refresher cycle for every hiab operator; signed competence attestations.
controls.uk_regulation: ALLMI Operator Cert + LOLER 1998 Reg 9 (Competence)

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Operator competence is an ISO 42001 control.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-operator signed competence attestation: ALLMI cert-id, issue date, expiry, refresher completed.

controls.title: CPA Hire vs Contract Lift triage (Baldwins £951k case avoidance)
controls.description: Auto-classify every hire as CPA Hire (insurance burden on hirer) vs Contract Lift (insurance on supplier); generate matching T&C bundle.
controls.uk_regulation: CPA Model Conditions of Hire + Baldwins v Bradshaw (Northern) Ltd [2018] EWHC

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Contractual model is the primary risk-treatment choice for crane operations.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-hire signed triage attestation with chosen model, rationale, T&C version, customer acceptance hash.

controls.title: AI risk-management system (EU AI Act Art 9)
controls.description: For every AI system in scope (e.g., routing optimisation, driver scoring), maintain a continuous risk-management process per EU AI Act Art 9.
controls.uk_regulation: EU AI Act Art 9 + ISO/IEC 23894:2023 (AI risk mgmt guidance)

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Direct compliance.
ISO 42001 risk planning = EU AI Act Art 9 risk management.
Whole NIST AI RMF lifecycle covers EU AI Act Art 9 requirements.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-AI-system signed risk register: identified risks, severity, treatment, review-cadence, owner.

controls.title: Training-data governance (EU AI Act Art 10)
controls.description: Documented data-governance regime for training / validation / test sets: relevance, representativeness, bias detection + mitigation.
controls.uk_regulation: EU AI Act Art 10 (Data + data governance) + GDPR Art 5 (Data minimisation)

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Direct compliance.
ISO 42001 explicitly requires data quality management.
Maps directly.
Training-data governance is also a GDPR controller obligation.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-dataset signed data-card: provenance, schema, bias-test results, retention policy, lawful basis under GDPR.

controls.title: Human oversight measures (EU AI Act Art 14)
controls.description: Design + document the human-oversight mechanisms for each high-risk AI system; signed attestation for every override + intervention.
controls.uk_regulation: EU AI Act Art 14 + UK Data (Use + Access) Act 2025 s.80 (ADM)

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Direct compliance.
Human oversight is the inverse of solely-automated; required where decisions affect individuals legally.
Maps directly.
Defines oversight roles.

controls.evidence_pattern: Signed per-intervention log: decision-id, AI recommendation, human reviewer-id, override rationale (if any), final decision.

controls.title: Transparency notice to data subjects (EU AI Act Art 13 + GDPR Art 13/14)
controls.description: Publish clear transparency notice for any AI-influenced decision (driver scoring, route optimisation, hiring) covering both EU AI Act Art 13 + GDPR.
controls.uk_regulation: EU AI Act Art 13 + GDPR Art 13 + Art 14

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Direct compliance.
AI transparency builds on GDPR transparency obligations.
UK transparency notice requirement.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-AI-system signed transparency notice version + hash; per-decision attestation references the notice version in effect.

controls.title: Accuracy + robustness + cybersecurity testing (EU AI Act Art 15)
controls.description: Documented testing regime for AI accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity; pen-tests + adversarial tests on signed cadence.
controls.uk_regulation: EU AI Act Art 15 + NIS2 (where applicable)

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Direct compliance.
Maps to ISO 42001 V&V + security controls.
Direct mapping.
Test cadence + change discipline = SOC 2 territory.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-release signed test report: accuracy metrics, robustness probes (perturbation tests), pen-test summary, sign-off by qualified tester.

controls.title: Solely-automated decision notice + opt-out (UK DUAA 2025 s.80)
controls.description: For any solely-automated decision with legal/significant effect (driver hire/fire, insurance pricing, route discipline), provide notice + opt-out per UK Data (Use + Access) Act 2025 s.80.
controls.uk_regulation: UK Data (Use + Access) Act 2025 s.80 (replacing UK GDPR Art 22)

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Direct UK compliance.

frameworks_satisfied.clause: Art 22 (Predecessor regime; EU operators still apply)
frameworks_satisfied.rationale: EU equivalent.
Deployers must enable affected persons to exercise rights.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-decision-class signed policy: scope, notice text, opt-out mechanism, human-review escalation; per-individual attestation when opt-out exercised.

controls.title: AI serious-incident reporting (EU AI Act Art 73)
controls.description: Detect + report AI serious incidents to the relevant market-surveillance authority within EU AI Act Art 73 timelines.
controls.uk_regulation: EU AI Act Art 73 + GDPR Art 33 (Personal-data breach 72h)

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Direct compliance.
Maps to incident response.
Direct mapping.
Personal-data-touching incidents trigger GDPR too.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-incident signed dossier: detection time, classification (serious / non-serious), affected systems, reported-to + reported-at, remediation.

controls.title: GDPR Article 22 / DUAA s.80 ADM safeguards
controls.description: Implement safeguards around any automated decision-making affecting data subjects: meaningful information about the logic, significance, consequences.
controls.uk_regulation: UK Data (Use + Access) Act 2025 s.80 + GDPR Art 22

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Direct compliance.
UK-specific replacement.
AI Act builds on GDPR explainability.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-decision-pipeline signed model card: input features, logic summary, significance assessment, consequences, opt-out / human-review path.

controls.title: DPIA + FRIA for high-risk processing
controls.description: Conduct + sign Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) + Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment (FRIA) per EU AI Act Art 27 before deploying high-risk AI.
controls.uk_regulation: GDPR Art 35 (DPIA) + EU AI Act Art 27 (FRIA)

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Direct compliance.
Direct compliance — FRIA required for high-risk AI deployment.
Maps to ISO 42001 impact assessment.
Risk-impact mapping is NIST AI RMF core.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-deployment signed DPIA + FRIA: rights affected, mitigations, residual risk acceptance by accountable owner.

controls.title: Data Subject Access Request (DSAR) handling
controls.description: Receive + respond to GDPR Art 15 access requests within 1 month; maintain signed audit trail of every DSAR.
controls.uk_regulation: UK GDPR Art 15 + UK DPA 2018

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Direct compliance.
DSARs sit under Privacy criteria.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-DSAR signed dossier: request-id, requester verification, data scope, response date, redactions, lawful-basis citations.

controls.title: GDP cold-chain temperature excursion handling
controls.description: Monitor + signed-log every temperature excursion in pharma cold-chain; route to Qualified Person for sign-off + impact assessment.
controls.uk_regulation: MHRA Good Distribution Practice (2013/C 343/01) s.5 + WDA(H) licence conditions

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Excursion = monitored anomaly + incident workflow.
Cold chain is an availability + detection control.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-excursion signed event: time-temp curve, duration, products affected, QP review-id, disposition (release / quarantine / destroy).

controls.title: Livestock welfare in transit (>8h journey)
controls.description: For journeys >8h with live animals: vehicle approval, journey log, rest periods, water + feed intervals; signed per-journey attestation.
controls.uk_regulation: EU Reg 1/2005 + Welfare of Animals (Transport) (England) Order 2006

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Standardised journey logging.
Journey records must be complete + accurate.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-journey signed log: species, headcount, vehicle approval-id, start/end times, rest stops, water/feed events, arrival condition.

controls.title: UAS commercial drone authorisation (open / specific / certified)
controls.description: Track operator authorisation + per-flight authorisation for commercial drone use; signed flight logs.
controls.uk_regulation: CAP 722 + UK CAA Regulation 2019/947 Open/Specific/Certified categories

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Per-flight planning is operational control.

controls.evidence_pattern: Per-flight signed log: pilot-id, operator-authorisation, category, mission, geofence, max altitude, post-flight status.

controls.title: Audit ledger integrity (signed hash chain)
controls.description: Every signed attestation chains via HMAC-SHA256 (prev_hash + canonical_payload); the chain is independently verifiable at the public verifier endpoint.
controls.uk_regulation: Cross-cutting — supports all of the above as evidence-integrity primitive

frameworks_satisfied.rationale: Tamper-evident logging is core ISO 42001.
Hash-chained audit log = continuous monitoring + integrity.
Audit ledger IS the tracking mechanism.

controls.evidence_pattern: Continuous append-only HMAC chain; any tamper breaks chain_intact=false; verifier replays the chain on demand.
